You know, what a harsh environment that's going to be and you're upset over a single swear word during my passionate exuberance talking about this business. I'm not sure you're ready for this business and how much we're going to be doing together. That's how you get frame control over somebody who is going to call you out for being an authentic, passionate, feeling human being. All right? Make sense to you? Okay. Where do we go? How do you get the frame back once the frame's been broken? Yes. So this is really a great question. All right. As good as again, I can tell you guys about all these things and all these great things I've done but I have lost dozens of deals and I've made really terrible mistakes in boardrooms and my one claim to infamy is I lost a billion dollar deal that we otherwise would have gotten. Okay? But I screwed it up. It's funny, a film crew was doing a little documentary on me and they wanted to talk to some of the people from my past. Right? And I introduced them to the good people and also the bad people, you know, people who don't have great things to say about me. Right? And the bad people, they all say, yeah, that bastard, or he blew up a billion dollar deal. We're going to make a lot of money on it. Right? But even the good people who love me, he said that bastard, or he blew up that billion dollar deal so you can lose the frame and you can do wrong things. So what I have learned when you really need the frame back, what you need to do is perpetrate a denial or a defiance or a denial or defiance. And I give the example in the book but let's review it here in person. I almost always leave something big, something big and gnarly and physical and multi-dimensional there on the desk near the person, you know, who we're presenting to. And it's bait. Right? So they have their name on it. You say, submarine plans for Jeff. Jeff will not live another day without knowing the information in this book. Something that we know they're going to reach for. And at that moment we look for it. When they reach for it, we go, boom. I tell you when you can look at this because this is my meeting. We're on my agenda. We start when I say start and we stop when I say stop. And when it's time and I say so, then I'm going to show this to you and you're sure to love it because this has some really cool stuff that we've prepared for you. But it's not time yet. Let's go on to the next thing that I have planned for you. Right? And so that's one very, very meaningful way that we can get the frame back when it's taken away from us. All right? Now a second way, a second way that we can do this is a two-step process. One you have to identify what's happening. Before you react, try and figure out, is this a power frame? What just happened? Right? Did they use the morality frame on me? How did my frame get broken? What is this? Give these things names. Once you name things, then you can figure out how to deal with them. All right? So for example, one way somebody can break your frame is they can say, I'm very sorry. I know you're in the middle of your presentation. I have to take a call. It's an emergency. It's my wife, dog, cat, rabbit, secretary, car, whatever. And you're maybe at the crescendo of your pitch. It's going to be very hard to build up all that momentum again. Boom. Frame broken. They don't even wait for you to approve and they leave the room. They come back 15 minutes later and go, where were we? Maybe we should start from the beginning. I got a little distracted. So that's as broken as a frame can get. So for me, now I've got to start working hard. I don't just go, well, okay, I'll start from the beginning and do this little song and dance again and stand on my hands and light my feet on fire and dance around for you. All right. I might check in and say, Bob, everything's settled. Can we, what I'd like you to do is say to me, Oren, I like you and I like the basics of this deal and I want to continue. And he'll go, yeah, of course, we're totally into it. Let's keep going. I go, no. I want you to say to me, Oren. I like you. It's important for me to hear that. I'm very interested in this deal and I want to continue. We have talked a lot about the power frame, right? And this is probably one that you are most familiar with. We all encounter it. Walk in a room, any room, any business trying to sell something, boom. You are faced with the power frame because they have the money. Where does the power frame really come from? What are the roots of it? What's the source? It comes from their other people's status, status. And status comes from money, celebrity, and influence. This is not my personal insight, although I agree with it. Cognitive psychologists hook people up to these functional magnetic resonance machines, FMRIs. And they show people these things, money and they show celebrities and they show people great influence and they see how the brain lights up and what areas. And they know what status comes from. And status comes from money, celebrity comes from influence. A high status guy that I've done business with, Ivan Alexander. He has more than a billion dollars. He has a lot of money. That's where his status comes from. He comes from his money. Give you another example. Morgan Freeman has celebrity. Morgan Freeman from all those movies where aliens invade and he's the black president. Telling people what to do. Running for their lives. Anyway, Morgan Freeman has celebrity. That's where his status comes from. David Petraeus is the director of the CIA, the central intelligence agency. And he has influence, lots of influence. Money, celebrity, influence. That's where status comes from and power is driven by status. Just like Scarface. You've seen that, you know how it works. The power frame, why is it so, why do you have to combat it? Why do you care about it? Because those who exhibit the power frame want economic value from you. They want concessions from you in exchange for things that cost them nothing. Let's pause on this for a minute. I want to say it again. The power frame wants economic value from you. It wants you to lower your price or give more or suffer economically in exchange for nothing. It is not a value exchange. They're using power to extract value from you. That's why we have to deal with it. That's why we have to close distance and put it down when we encounter it. But unlike the way we do it in the price frame, it's not so easy. So what do they want value for? Sometimes just their name, hey, you're doing business with Donald Trump. Therefore, you can tell everyone you did deal with Donald Trump. Give Donald a lower price. So for Donald's name or for doing business with Walmart, they want a lower price. That's the power frame and you've got to take it away because you want to pay for their name. It's not economic value. You may rationalize that it is, but it's not usually. You'll pay people for their name. Some people will say, hey, we'll let you up to our field of play. You can tell people you're doing deals with Donald Trump. You're doing deals with Target. You're doing deals with General Electric. You're on that field of play now. You've got to earn your way into that field of play. Oh, great. How do I earn my way in? Oh, by lowering my price. I don't want to do that. So I've got to crush your power frame. All right? Sometimes these guys will use the power frame in this way and say, we have the ability to order large volumes of your product. So come to us and lower your price because we have the ability to order large volumes. It's not a contract. It's not even a promise. It's just the ability. So when somebody tries to power frame you like that, close distance, close it down. All right? How do you do that? How do you deal with these power frames? As we said in the book, Pitch Anything, we'll say it again here, with small denials and defiances. A small denial and a small defiance, but they must be social in nature. Can't just defy people in a way that's shrill or bitter or cruel. You do it in a fun way. You smile and it's social. Let's talk about doing that right now and give an example. So maybe the easiest way to exhibit a defiance is to take control of the agenda away. So maybe you're in a meeting with people and you're both kind of deciding on the agenda, right? And it's kind of rolling. If you take the agenda away and make it yours, that's a nice defiance. How do you do this? So guys who have the power frame, they always make social missteps. They're clumsy. They always are. Think about it. It's king-like behavior. They always make social missteps. Oh, take the peasants' land away, right? These king-like behaviors and they will always do something weird and make a misstep. Here's an example you hear all the time. Oh, the power frame will say, forget telling us your background. Just give me the price, right? Well that's rude and it's asocial. When somebody does that, it's okay to call them out. You say, stop. Come on. You know how this works? We give you our background. You hear about our product and you ask us about price and there's a way these things work, right? Looks like I have to take over the agenda to make this run properly. This is now my meeting and it's running on my agenda and here's how we're going to go. I'm going to give myself some time to present the background. I'm going to tell you all about the product and then I'm going to give you some time to ask questions about price and then we're going to keep going on my agenda for the next hour. This is my meeting. I say when it starts and I say when it stops and when the bathroom break comes, I'll let you know when you can have one. But you got to say that with a smile. You're never going to turn to anybody and say in the business world, you might not turn to a billionaire and with a frown on your face say, you sit there until I tell you and when it's time for a bathroom break, I'll tell you when to go. You're going to call security and throw you out. But if you say in the way I say it, this is my meeting. We're on my agenda. I'll let you guys know when it's time for a bathroom break. You pause and smile and do the social weight. It can be fun. At least you've got the agenda. You've created that denial. We have in the past talked about shorter time constraints. Shorter time constraints. That's another way to get control and to come up against the power frame. You'll hear a lot, hey guys, I'm running a little fast today, the power frame will say. I'm running a little fast today and things are backing up. I only have about 45 minutes for this meeting. You'll say 45 minutes, man, you guys waste a lot of time. We are only playing 35 minutes to do this. You'll say we only have 35 minutes even though you have 45. It seems like you guys are running slow. You do a shorter time constraint. You take the power frame away a little bit. Definitely you want to take away money as a source of power. If a guy is grinding you on price with the power frame, grinding you on price, you say, whoa, whoa, stop, stop, stop. Listen, money is not that important to us. We have all the money in the world. We're well-financed. We do great with our product. What's important to us is good partners in having fun. All this grinding on price, it's not fun. You're going to take money away as a source of power. Many times in the business that I'm in, you'll see the power frame talk about all these accoutrements of power, all these things they have, a plane, the access to nice restaurants, big conference rooms, different things like that. You're going to take away the accoutrements of power and rely on basic high-quality business principles, the fortune cookie wisdom of business. I work hard. I don't waste money. We get rest at the end of the day. We build quality products. We choose good customers. We don't rush into things we don't know about. We focus on what we're good at. You're going to reframe all the fancy things that power frame has, saying, I don't care about that because we go by good business principles. Here's how that's done. I hear all the time, hey, ride with us on our plane. I know what that is. They want to show off how much power they have and how much money. They can get a concession from me. I'll say, no. Our policy is to travel coach. We put our money into our product so we can give better value to our customer. We don't fly around in first class or pride at planes. The truth is, we do have a plane, but we use it for utility, not to show off to people. You can't get to three cities in one day without a plane. It's very difficult. If somebody says, hey, come ride on our plane and they're trying to show off and get price concessions from you, you're going to say, no. We're happy to travel coach. We want to put our money into our product. When people invite you out to a very high-end restaurant, you can say, as I do, we'd rather stay in a working environment. Why don't we order a couple sandwiches from Subway? I'll let you pay for those, though, because I appreciate your generosity. Let's stay in a working environment and focus and getting stuff done. That's fortune cookie wisdom over showing off. That's how you disrupt these power frame behaviors. Many times, they will want to talk about their grand history. We've been around since the 80s when we bought a $100 million business. Then in the 90s, we became the number one firm to blah, blah, blah. In 2000, we became even better than the number one, the number 0.5 firm. We just became greater than amazing. Then in 2007, when everybody else was suffering, they'll say, we thought amazing was great, but we became intergalactic stellar universal rulers of everything that moves. We know this better than everybody. If you work with us, you'll be able to access this grand history that we have, this amazing awesomeness that we are. When they say that to you, give you their whole history and the billions that they've done and all these great things, you say, you know, that's awesome and I'm glad to hear about those things, but I think it's better to focus on the present time and current events because what we're doing isn't based in the past. It's based in right now. Let's focus on the present time and current events. The stuff that you've done, it's just not a predictor of future performance. As you know, any stock offering says that past performance is not a prediction of future performance and I feel the same way. I think it's best if we all just focus on current events. You'll say something like that when they give you their grand history. What you will always experience from the power frame is the game of fetch me another rock. You'll make your presentation, your proposal, you'll do a great job of pitching, you'll get them wanting it and they'll say like this, we love what you have and we're ready to buy it. If only you could bring me a blue rock, I love everything you rock. You'll go, oh, that's all you need is a blue rock and then you run away and you find one and you bring it back and you go, here's a blue rock and they go, that's awesome. Thank you. I love this blue rock. We want to buy it even more now. The only thing we though we need is a green rock with polka dots on it. That's all you need? They go, yeah, we're done. Bring it to us and we're done. You run off and you do whatever it takes, you come back and you go, okay, Mr. Powerframe, billionaire, buyer, company, person with all the power, I have brought you what you seek, green rock with polka dots on it and they look at it and they say, this is the best green rock with polka dots on it I've ever seen in my life and we thank you so much for bringing it and we are ready now to close. All we need is a red rock, a red rock. Some people will feel like they're so close and they're now used to fetching another rock and they know where to go get red rock so they'll go get it. When you encounter that, you're going to say, stop, rock fetching is over. I will in fact, if you want to close, go get you the red rock that you require but it's now time for you to fetch me a rock. You're going to issue a letter of intent, a memorandum of understanding, a purchase and sale agreement, a sample contract, you're going to issue a purchase order, a draft purchase order, you're going to pay for that red rock by advancing in the deal and that's how you deal with the fetch me another rock. At some point you stop doing it and you say, I will fetch you this rock you require but you are going to also fetch me a rock. It's very important that you learn to do this. You're going to hear promises of we could introduce you to Donald Trump and Warren Buffett and the CEO of Target and Mick Jagger. Is Mick Jagger still alive? He is. Okay. Introduce you to Mick Jagger and all these great people. We could do this for you. If you only give us these concessions we're asking for and obviously the response to that you know it by now. Hey guys, we're very focused on our current plan. All that stuff sounds wonderful and let's get into that someday because we would love to meet Warren Buffett and do business with him and sell our stuff into Target. We'd love to go to the moon with you as you suggested we do but for the moment we're focused on our current plan so let's just do that. People hear from the power frame oh we made such and such company that we also gave in order to a success. So if you work with us, if you sell to us we'll make you a success too just by doing business with us. Or we can order more from you in the future as we've said before. Obviously you're just going to say then yes, yes, that's great and everything. Tell the power frame yes. But those were fortunate and unique circumstances and quite a lot has changed in the world. So there's no way of knowing what's going to happen in the future. Let's just focus on the deal we have on the table without these external situations. Let's just focus on what we have. We don't need you to make us a success. We're already a success. Let's do a value exchange. So again from the power frame you're always going to hear we get better pricing because of who we are, our name and what we can do for you. You're just going to say it's better to focus on value. What we have is not a discount product. If you want a discount product there are cheaper products and there are cheaper services and there are cheaper deals. There's cheaper than us. Look, if you want a cheaper deal then you should go get it. I'll refer you to the cheap one but that's not the right way. What we have is value. We're charging for value. So if you want cheap you want to buy it at a discount warehouse we'll tell you where to go find that. But for us we're priced to value. So let me know which way do you want to go, value or you want cheap. That's how you're going to deal with that. These are lots and lots of ways you deal with the power frame. We're going to list them all out for you and give you scripts so you can do this step by step. There's one other thing you need to remember. Whenever you're facing power, real power and status, money, influence, celebrity, your natural reaction is to act needy or to be star struck. What I'm going to instruct you to do here is to never be needy and be non-reactive. When they continue to pull these kind of power moves you're just not going to react. So if they say let's go to a nice restaurant, we'd rather stay here and focus on the work. Let's order in from Subway. Well let's go to a nice restaurant and then I'll take you on the plane. That sounds nice but really if we have to go anywhere we just fly coach and we put all our money into the product. Let's go to a restaurant. I'll take you on the plane. I'll drive you around in my Bentley and we'll promise to give you a big order and we'll introduce you to Warren Buffett and then we'll walk you over to the Target Purchasing Department and then later in the day we'll take you to the dark side of the moon which no human has ever stepped foot on. It will be so amazing you'll lose your minds. You in light of all these promises and these fancy things will say let's just focus on what we have. We're interested in doing this deal. It's a good starting point and then we'll move on from there and hopefully grow the relationship. You are not needy to these things and you are non-reactive. When you're non-reactive this disrupts the power frame. They don't see the response that they're used to getting. That's how it disrupts it. They offer a restaurant and a giant conference room and free Mac laptops and to take you to the dark side of the moon where no human has ever stepped foot. To take you back in time to the Renaissance period. You can marry ten wives and watch beautiful paintings happen and real jousting and then they offer you that time machine to take you into the future. You can see how the world has evolved a hundred years from now and then Steve Jobs is there reincarnated from his DNA. You get to meet him a hundred years from now when they offer you that. You're going to disrupt it because they're going to expect you to supplicate and fall into that and they will try harder and offer you more and more. They'll offer you more and more. They'll continue to apply the power frame to the point that it becomes awkward. What else can they offer besides taking you in the future to meet Steve Jobs a hundred years from now? Meet President Bush a thousand years from now? What else can they offer? They'll continue to offer crazy stuff. That's when you'll know when it becomes awkward and they're using and trying to use more and more power that you have frame control. You've disrupted the power frame. You have frame control. You're non-reactive frame and you're fortune cookie business wisdom and you're non-neediness and all the things we talked about here have come together and you have frame control. This is fun stuff. I love it. Just wait. In the strong method, you're only at S. The next steps for you, you're going to go through the companion material here in the framing section. You're going to go through the workbooks. You're going to do the scripts like we talked about. You're going to go through the additional videos. It's definitely worth to reread chapter two in Pitch Anything. Then when you're fully immersed in the technology of frames and framing and you feel like when you hear somebody say something negative about your price frame, you close distance and shut it down and you have the skills to do that and you know how to deal with the power frame. You know how to recognize an analyst frame. You know what to do when you see it. Then you're ready for the next module. The next module in strong is T, telling the story. This is awesome. When you bring your framing skills and you combine them with the ability to tell a great business story or a narrative, your pitch to close ratio will start going up and you're just at T in strong. Already, your pitch to close ratio will start going up. That's what we're here to do together.
